cases.title: USB fan warms, does not spin, and changes when the wire is moved
cases.truth_basis: This is a low-voltage motor gadget where the known evidence is enough for a real diagnosis: supply voltage stays up, the symptom changes when the harness is moved, the operator confirmed cracked solder at the motor harness, and reflow made the fan spin normally.

assistant_oracle_deduction.most_likely_fault: connector, solder joint, or motor harness intermittency
reasoning: A 4.91 V USB input during attempted startup argues against the USB supply collapsing as the primary fault.
Behavior changes when the wire is moved, which strongly points to the harness, connector, crimp, or solder joint.
The operator confirmed cracked solder at the motor harness.
Reflowing the harness joint fixed the fan, so the diagnosis is confirmed by outcome.
assistant_oracle_deduction.correct_action: reflow or repair the harness/connector solder joint, then add strain relief and verify continuity under flex.

system_dossier_summary.executive_summary: USB fan warms but motor will not spin value trial has 1 grounded claim(s) and 3 weak claim(s). 2 evidence task(s) remain open.

Resolved evidence: operator confirmed cracked solder at motor harness
Resolved evidence: operator attached harness, connector, and solder-joint closeups

2 open evidence task(s) must be resolved before strong certainty
next_actions: Resolve evidence for: Device family: small_dc_motor_gadget
Resolve evidence for: Top fault candidate: Driver stage or motor/load path fault
Resolve evidence for: 2 open evidence task(s) must be resolved before strong certainty

cases.title: Electric toothbrush not charging through its dock
cases.truth_basis: This checks whether the system handles a battery/charging gadget instead of a motor fan. It has a measured dock output, close-up evidence, a repair outcome, and remaining charge-current uncertainty.

assistant_oracle_deduction.most_likely_fault: charging dock/contact/input path or battery charge path
reasoning: The dock output measurement shows the dock produces about 5 V with no toothbrush seated.
Charging failures still need contact condition, charge current, and battery voltage checks before blaming a cell or charge controller.
The session is useful, but not fully closed because charge-current/current-draw evidence remains open.
assistant_oracle_deduction.correct_action: verify dock/contact cleanliness, measure battery voltage and charge current, then decide whether the dock path or battery pack is responsible.

system_dossier_summary.executive_summary: Electric toothbrush not charging end-to-end smoke has 1 grounded claim(s) and 3 weak claim(s). 3 evidence task(s) remain open.

Resolved evidence: dock contact and corrosion close-up smoke evidence

3 open evidence task(s) must be resolved before strong certainty

cases.title: Xbox controller stick drift
cases.truth_basis: This checks a customer-common repair lane with known expected actions: inspect/clean/replace the analog stick module and validate with controller tester readings.

assistant_oracle_deduction.most_likely_fault: analog stick, trigger, or button contact fault
assistant_oracle_deduction.correct_action: record before/after controller tester readings, inspect the joystick module and contacts, clean if possible, replace the stick module if drift remains.

repair_guide.top_fault_name: Analog stick, trigger, or button contact fault

first_tasks.prompt: before/after controller tester readings for stick center, axis range, triggers, and buttons
measure input connector polarity and voltage
connector closeups with wire colors and labels
top-side and bottom-side board photos
closeups of analog stick modules, button membranes, flex cables, and PCB contacts

assertions.description: System selects the controller input lane.
System identifies analog stick/contact fault.
System asks for before/after controller tester readings.
System requests closeups of the input assemblies.
System considers this lane solvable now.

cases.title: Laptop will not turn on
cases.truth_basis: This checks whether the system can be honest on a harder board-level case: laptop no-power is meaningful, but model-specific schematics/boardviews are needed for deeper diagnosis.

assistant_oracle_deduction.most_likely_fault: power input, protection, charger, or main rail fault
assistant_oracle_deduction.correct_action: start with known-good charger, charge port inspection, input fuse/main rail resistance, input voltage, and rail-to-ground resistance; do not claim model-specific board repair without schematic/boardview.

repair_guide.top_fault_name: Power input, protection, or regulator fault

first_tasks.prompt: input fuse or main-rail resistance with power disconnected
input voltage measurement
rail-to-ground resistance before power-up
connector closeups with wire colors and labels
top-side and bottom-side board photos

blockers: boardview/schematic and model-specific teardown needed for deeper board repair

assertions.description: System selects laptop power path.
System identifies power input/regulator fault.
System asks for input and rail resistance measurements.
System marks the case assistive only.
System calls out boardview/schematic as a blocker.

cases.title: Coffee maker not hot enough
cases.truth_basis: This checks high-voltage/mains honesty. The right behavior is to identify the heater/thermal chain but gate the workflow behind safety checks.

assistant_oracle_deduction.most_likely_fault: thermal fuse, thermostat, heating element, or control relay/triac fault
assistant_oracle_deduction.correct_action: unplug, verify discharge/isolation, check thermal fuse/thermostat continuity, measure heating element resistance against wattage, and avoid live mains probing.

repair_guide.top_fault_name: Heating element, thermal fuse, thermostat, or control relay fault

playbook.pattern_label: Mains heater or TV backlight safety triage

first_tasks.prompt: confirm appliance is unplugged and capacitors are discharged before continuity checks
thermal fuse and thermostat continuity readings
heating element resistance compared with expected wattage
confirm safe isolation, discharge state, and no live high-voltage probing before continuing
connector closeups with wire colors and labels

blockers: model-specific knowledge required before customer-facing claim
trained safety workflow required for high-voltage or mains portions

assertions.description: System selects mains heater appliance.
System identifies heater thermal cutoff/control fault.
System marks safety risk as high.
System requires unplug/discharge confirmation.
System asks for thermal fuse and heating element checks.
System does not call this broadly solved.

cases.title: Cordless drill battery will not charge
cases.truth_basis: This is another battery lane but not a toothbrush. The expected workflow is charger/contact/pack voltage checks before deciding charger vs pack fault.

assistant_oracle_deduction.most_likely_fault: battery, charge contact, charger, or charge-controller fault
assistant_oracle_deduction.correct_action: check contacts, verify charger, measure pack voltage, then inspect terminals/protection path.

repair_guide.top_fault_name: Battery, charge contact, or charge-controller fault

first_tasks.prompt: charger rating and no-load output voltage
battery pack voltage compared with nominal rating
charge current or dock current draw during safe charging test
connector closeups with wire colors and labels
top-side and bottom-side board photos

assertions.description: System selects battery charging gadget.
System identifies battery charge path fault.
System asks for charger rating/no-load voltage.
System asks for battery pack voltage.
System considers this lane solvable now.

cases.title: TV has sound but no picture
cases.truth_basis: This checks a high-voltage backlight/power workflow. The right response is backlight/power suspicion plus safety gating and model-specific limits.

assistant_oracle_deduction.most_likely_fault: backlight LED strip, backlight driver, or power-board fault
assistant_oracle_deduction.correct_action: do flashlight test, inspect/model the power board and backlight connectors, and only measure rails if trained for TV high-voltage work.

repair_guide.top_fault_name: Backlight LED strip, inverter/driver, or power-supply fault

playbook.pattern_label: Mains heater or TV backlight safety triage

first_tasks.prompt: standby/main rail readings only if trained for high-voltage TV work
flashlight-test result showing whether a faint image is present
power-board and backlight connector photos with model labels
confirm safe isolation, discharge state, and no live high-voltage probing before continuing
measure input connector polarity and voltage

blockers: model-specific knowledge required before customer-facing claim
trained safety workflow required for high-voltage or mains portions

assertions.description: System selects TV backlight/power lane.
System identifies backlight/power supply fault.
System marks safety risk as high.
System asks for flashlight-test evidence.
System asks for power-board/backlight connector photos.
System marks the case assistive only.
